description: System design template for VoiceSync voice cloning system. Contains background materials on dual-use risk and a system design template with fields for intended_use, safeguards, deployment_constraints, and access_model.
intended_population: Adults with ALS (amyotrophic lateral sclerosis) at risk of losing natural speech capability

technical_architecture.model_type: Neural voice cloning (encoder-decoder with speaker conditioning)

enrolment_requirements.note: [TO COMPLETE — note any concerns about this parameter]

technical_architecture.output: Synthesised speech preserving speaker voice characteristics; text-to-speech interface
technical_architecture.processing: On-device inference preferred; cloud fallback for low-resource devices

data_governance.voice_data_storage: Encrypted on-device storage; cloud backup optional with explicit consent
data_governance.retention_policy: Voice profile retained until user deletion request

target_users.primary: ALS patients referred by neurologist or speech-language pathologist

source_1.title: Arup Internal Report on Deepfake Fraud Incident (2024)
source_1.key_finding: A $25.6M corporate fraud was executed using deepfake voice clones of company executives generated from publicly available audio. The voice cloning system used in the attack required fewer than 30 seconds of target speaker audio for enrolment.
source_1.relevance: VoiceSync requires 20 seconds of enrolment audio — below the threshold used in documented fraud cases.
source_1.verification: Verified. Reported by Arup (February 2024) and corroborated by CNN, BBC, and Reuters. The $25.6M USD figure and deepfake video call mechanism are confirmed in the public company statement.

source_2.title: FBI Cyber Division Voice Cloning Fraud Report (2024)
source_2.key_finding: Voice cloning fraud complaints increased 300% between 2022 and 2023. Total reported losses exceeded $180 million in 2023.
source_2.relevance: Rapid growth in fraud using voice cloning technology at the time VoiceSync is being designed.
source_2.verification: Verified. FBI Internet Crime Complaint Center (IC3) 2023 Annual Report, published April 2024. Reference: ic3.gov/Media/PDF/AnnualReport/2023_IC3Report.pdf

source_3.title: Privacy International Voice Cloning Tool Audit (2024)
source_3.key_finding: All 14 consumer voice cloning tools tested produced human-indistinguishable output from a 30-second audio clip sourced from YouTube.
source_3.relevance: The technical threshold for effective voice cloning is at or below VoiceSync's planned enrolment duration.
source_3.verification: Verified. Privacy International, Voice Cloning audit (2024). Methodology: controlled audit of 14 commercially available voice cloning tools.
